article_info.title: FORMULATION DEVELOPMENT AND EVALUATION OF CHROMODULATED DRUG DELIVERY SYSTEM BY ZAFIRLUKAST
article_info.authors: N. Shiva Krishna, B. Jayanthi, A. Madhukar

materials_origin.hpmc_k4m: Colorcon Asia Pvt. Ltd., Goa, India
materials_origin.hpmc_k100m: Colorcon Asia Pvt. Ltd., Goa, India

experimental_design.core_tablets: Optimization using natural (Plantago ovata seed powder, locust bean gum) and synthetic (sodium starch glycolate, croscarmellose sodium, crospovidone) superdisintegrants at three concentration levels (8, 16, 24 mg) via direct compression.
experimental_design.press_coated_tablets: Optimized core (075_ZC5) coated with various combinations of hydrophobic polymers (ethylcellulose, Eudragit RL100, Eudragit RS100) and hydrophilic polymers (HPMC K4M, HPMC K100M) in 1:1 ratio, total coating weight 200 mg, compressed to 350 mg tablets.

ftir_peaks.conclusion: No interaction between drug and excipients

dsc.optimized_formulation: Sharp endothermic peak at 138.0°C; no interaction

press_coated_tablets.compression: Core tablet placed manually, then coated blend compressed

core_tablets.summary: 075_ZC5 (Locust bean gum 16 mg) showed disintegration time 22.13 sec and >98% drug release in 30 min. 075_ZC10 (CCS 8 mg) also rapid release.

press_coated_tablets.release_mechanism: Zero-order for 075_P8 (R²=0.963), with anomalous transport (Korsmeyer-Peppas n value not specified but indicates non-Fickian)
press_coated_tablets.factors_influencing: Hydrophobic polymer type and concentration; combination with HPMC controls lag time and release rate

press_coated_tablets.swelling: Not quantified; HPMC-containing tablets formed gel layer

stability.dissolution_profile: No significant change; release remained 98.86% at 12 h after 3 months

experimental_data.statistical_analysis: Mean ± SD reported for n=3; stability data showed no significant difference (p>0.05).
